description_long: This is a hand axe featuring a robust, slightly curved wooden handle with a smooth finish and a natural wood grain pattern. The head is made of metal, with a broad, wedge-shaped blade on one side and a flat poll on the opposite end, suitable for splitting or chopping tasks. The blade has a single sharp edge and a pronounced beard, allowing for controlled, precise cuts. The handle is ergonomically shaped, tapering towards the end for a secure grip, and is attached to the head using a traditional wedged mounting. There is a visible logo or marking on the side of the handle, suggesting branding or manufacturer information. The overall design is utilitarian, typical of axes found in urban garages, workshops, or for park maintenance.
description: A hand axe with a curved wooden handle and a single-edged metal blade, featuring a visible logo on the handle. The design is practical and robust, suitable for chopping or splitting tasks.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the curved wooden handle and the broad metal blade with a visible logo on the handle.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the narrow profile of the blade and the rounded end of the handle.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the handle's curvature and the blade's wedge shape, with the poll visible at the back.
description_view_3: Bottom-up view focusing on the thickness of the handle and the blade's edge alignment.
attribute_handle_type: Curved wooden handle with a smooth finish, ergonomic taper, and oval cross-section for a secure grip.
attribute_blade_design: Single-edged, wedge-shaped steel blade with a pronounced beard and flat poll, no decorative features.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting securing the blade to the wooden handle.
